commit.message:
Add write operations gated by ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE (#12)

* Add write operations gated by ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE

Implement the full deposit write workflow, all behind an explicit opt-in:

  ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=true   (or '1') enables write tools
  ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=false  (default) — write tools not registered

## New tools (only registered when write is enabled)

Deposition CRUD:
  create_deposition   POST /api/deposit/depositions
  get_deposition      GET  /api/deposit/depositions/:id
  update_deposition   PUT  /api/deposit/depositions/:id  (metadata JSON)
  delete_deposition   DELETE /api/deposit/depositions/:id (drafts only)

File management:
  upload_file             PUT to bucket URL; accepts utf-8 or base64 content
  delete_deposition_file  DELETE /api/deposit/depositions/:id/files/:file_id

Deposition actions:
  publish_deposition   .../actions/publish
  edit_deposition      .../actions/edit  (unlock published record)
  discard_deposition   .../actions/discard
  new_version          .../actions/newversion

## Safety design

- Write tools are not registered at all when ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE is false,
  so AI agents never see them unless the operator has opted in.
- Each handler also checks the flag independently (defence-in-depth).
- All write methods call requireAuth() — clear error if key is missing.
- docker-compose.yml exposes ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE (default: false).

## Tests (test/zenodo-write.test.ts, 19 tests)

- Gate: write tools absent in tool list when disabled
- Gate: write tools present when ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE=true
- Read-only tools still present when write is enabled
- 10 defence-in-depth handler tests (each write tool returns isError when flag off)
- 2 not-authenticated tests (write enabled, no API key)
- 4 live sandbox round-trip tests (create → upload → update → delete),
  activated only when ZENODO_SANDBOX_API_KEY is set

CI: passes ZENODO_SANDBOX_API_KEY secret and sets ZENODO_ALLOW_WRITE
accordingly.

38/38 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback: SSRF fix, type safety, encoding validation, upload size limit, description fix

commit.message:
Build full-stack Custom Resume web application

- Backend: Node.js + Express + TypeScript API (port 3001)
  - In-memory CRUD for job applications (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/jobs)
  - POST /api/jobs/:id/tailor-resume — AI resume tailoring via OpenAI gpt-4o-mini
  - POST /api/jobs/:id/generate-cover-letter — cover letter generation with style matching
  - dotenv, cors, uuid, openai npm packages
  - Graceful error when OPENAI_API_KEY is not set

- Frontend: React + TypeScript + Vite (port 5173)
  - Job list view with cards showing company, title, date, AI completion badges
  - Job detail view with inline editing for all fields
  - ResumeEditor: paste base resume → AI-tailored version with copy button
  - CoverLetter: paste base resume + style sample → AI cover letter with copy button
  - Vite proxy forwards /api to backend
  - Plain CSS with responsive grid, no external CSS framework

- Infrastructure
  - .gitignore for node_modules, dist, .env
  - backend/.env.example with OPENAI_API_KEY and PORT
  - Comprehensive README with setup, API reference, dev/prod instructions
  - Both projects build cleanly with zero TypeScript errors

commit.message:
fix: use RemoteIpAddress for rate limiting, not spoofable X-Forwarded-For

X-Forwarded-For header was unconditionally trusted as the primary IP
source, allowing attackers to trivially bypass per-IP rate limiting by
spoofing the header. Now uses RemoteIpAddress directly — when behind a
reverse proxy, ASP.NET Core's ForwardedHeadersMiddleware should be
configured with KnownProxies, which rewrites RemoteIpAddress safely.

   - Central control flow connecting Modules 1, 2, 3
   - Monitors signal changes (detects BUY/SELL switches)

   - Thread-safe monitoring of positions and signals
   - Configurable signal check interval (30s) and position update (10s)
   - User-controllable auto-trading toggle

2. backend/mcp_server.py (Market Context Protocol)
   - Exposes market data as MCP resources (market:// URIs)
   - MCP tools: query_market_data, query_signals, query_positions, check_trading_rules
   - Enables external AI agents to query market state
   - Read-only access to prevent accidental data corruption
   - Resources: latest-prices, signals, positions, risk-status, account

3. backend/server.py - New endpoints:
   - GET /api/positions - open positions with P&L
   - GET /api/portfolio - account summary (equity, cash, daily loss)
   - GET /api/tax-log - trade history (JSON or CSV download)
   - POST /api/trade - manual trade execution
   - POST /api/toggle-auto-trade - user auto-trading control
   - GET /api/orchestrator-status - orchestrator state and signals
   - Orchestrator starts automatically as daemon thread

4. requirements.txt
   - Added mcp>=0.1.0 for Model Context Protocol support

Features:
✅ Signal-based trading with confidence filtering
✅ Automated position monitoring
✅ MCP market data exposure for AI integration
✅ User-controlled auto-trading toggle
✅ Full audit trail for compliance
✅ Real-time orchestration feedback

Architecture:
Module 1 (data) → Module 2 (signals) → Orchestrator → Module 3 (trading)

commit.message:
fix: allow embedded PDF viewer through X-Frame-Options (#1277)

* fix: allow embedded PDF viewer through X-Frame-Options (#1234)

Three root causes prevented some PDFs from displaying in the iframe
viewer while 'Open in new window' worked fine:

1. The @auth_error named location inherited the server-level
   X-Frame-Options: DENY.  When a session expired while viewing a PDF
   the 401 response was blocked inside the iframe.  Added explicit
   SAMEORIGIN + security headers to @auth_error in all three nginx
   configs (default.conf, default.conf.template, ssl.conf.template).

2. No proxy_hide_header for upstream X-Frame-Options.  If the backend
   ever sends its own header the browser would see conflicting values.
   Added proxy_hide_header X-Frame-Options in the /documents/ location
   so only nginx's SAMEORIGIN is sent.

3. resolveDocumentUrl() returned same-origin absolute URLs as-is when
   they lacked a /documents/ prefix (e.g. from DOCUMENT_URL_BASE).
   The iframe loaded the root location which has DENY.  Now same-origin
   URLs are re-routed through /documents/{token}.

Closes #1234

  exactly one entry keyed by the part name for a single-shape STEP.

* fix(container): disable BUILD_TESTING in Dockerfile; drop yaml-cpp from spack

include(CTest) implicitly sets BUILD_TESTING=ON, which causes cmake to
require yaml-cpp even though no tests are being built in the container.
Add -DBUILD_TESTING=OFF to avoid this.

yaml-cpp is a test-only dependency and is not present in the EIC spack
buildcache (ghcr.io/eic/spack-v2025.11.0), so spack had to build it
from source — lengthening the base-layer build unnecessarily.  Remove
it from spack.yaml so the base image only contains runtime dependencies
(Geant4 and OpenCASCADE).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci(container): cache spack base layer in GHCR by spack.yaml hash

base-container.yml:
- Hash .github/spack.yaml (first 8 hex chars of SHA-256) to produce a
  stable tag spack-<hash> that changes only when the environment changes.
- After GHCR login, inspect ghcr.io/eic/g4occt:spack-<hash> to detect a
  cache hit; skip the 44-min spack install + buildcache push on hit.
- On cache miss, push the new image under the hash tag (not :base directly)
  so the immutable content-addressed entry survives future retagging.
- Always retag the hash-tagged image as :base via docker buildx imagetools
  create (manifest-only, no layer download) so downstream steps find it.
- Add docker/setup-buildx-action@v3 (needed for imagetools create).

container.yml:
- Add cache-from/cache-to (type=registry, :buildcache tag) to
  build-push-action so Docker BuildKit layer cache is stored in GHCR and
  reused across builds when only the G4OCCT source changes.
